summary: Nexus is a researcher-process chassis: it combines a 45-stop project/exploration/academic pipeline, a Galaxy Map for cross-field mechanism discovery, upstream/downstream stale-state control, and room-specific instruments for source discovery, transfer testing, synthesis, drafting, provenance, and release.
final_use_case: A serious researcher can move from vague project idea to evidence-bound paper or proof artifact while the system records what each stage produced and what downstream work becomes stale if an upstream decision changes.

instruments.input: project state, source state, and prior-stage decisions
instruments.operation: turn an initial project idea into a bounded research task before substantive discovery begins; substage: Intake.
instruments.output: Intake work product and updated route state
instruments.upstream_dependency: previous pipeline stop and any source/method decisions it produced
instruments.downstream_use: next pipeline stop; later changes can mark dependent work stale
instruments.gap: needs a captured live walkthrough showing a real project moving through this stop
instruments.verification_check: Move one project through Intake stage; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

instruments.input: disciplines, subfields, candidate papers, scan results, field-status metrics, and candidate transfer arcs
instruments.operation: renders the source and idea terrain as discipline clusters, local neighborhoods, transfer arcs, and overlay layers so the researcher can look for non-obvious mechanism transfer rather than only keyword relevance
instruments.output: candidate research neighborhoods, cross-field routes, gap surfaces, and transfer candidates for Bridge and XPol testing
instruments.upstream_dependency: requires observatory scan state, candidate telemetry, field status, and source-pressure data
instruments.downstream_use: feeds Bridge mappings, XPol fit pressure, Evaluate source review, Crucible synthesis, and Forge drafting lanes
instruments.gap: needs a captured live research walkthrough showing a discovery moving from galaxy view to Bridge/XPol and then into a draftable research object
instruments.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (disciplines, subfields, candidate papers, scan...) produces output (candidate research neighborhoods, cross-field routes...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

instruments.instrument_name: Upstream / Downstream Dependency And Stale-State Atlas
instruments.input: academic stages, room dependency graph, upstream changes, downstream surfaces, stale counts, and blast-radius relationships
instruments.operation: marks later research stages as needing review when an upstream problem statement, method rule, source basis, or analytical result changes
instruments.output: dependency warnings, stale-stage flags, blast-radius cues, and revalidation tasks
instruments.upstream_dependency: requires project dependency atlas state and academic pipeline state
instruments.downstream_use: prevents literature review, methodology, analysis, discussion, conclusion, and release decisions from drifting out of alignment
instruments.gap: needs a public demonstration with one upstream revision and the resulting downstream recheck path
instruments.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (academic stages, room dependency graph, upstream...) produces output (dependency warnings, stale-stage flags, blast-radius...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

instruments.input: field scan, gap hypothesis, research stake, motive check, and early falsifier risk
instruments.operation: locates the project in a consequential problem space before the thesis hardens into a premature claim
instruments.output: frontier note naming field, gap pressure, motive for inquiry, and early falsifier risk
instruments.upstream_dependency: problem framing, source canon, method choices, findings, contradictions, and claim-level constraints
instruments.downstream_use: later academic stages, presentation readiness, Forge drafting, and public claim ceiling
instruments.gap: needs a captured live scholarly project walkthrough showing stage artifacts and stale-state propagation
instruments.verification_check: Move one project through Problem Exploration / Frontier Pressure Map; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

instruments.input: alignment, core claim or question, question quality, reasoning flow, scope boundary, falsifiers, and significance
instruments.operation: converts the explored terrain into a bounded research problem whose question can be answered, challenged, or revised
instruments.output: problem statement with scope boundary, question form, stakes, assumptions, and re-check triggers
instruments.upstream_dependency: problem framing, source canon, method choices, findings, contradictions, and claim-level constraints
instruments.downstream_use: later academic stages, presentation readiness, Forge drafting, and public claim ceiling
instruments.gap: needs a captured live scholarly project walkthrough showing stage artifacts and stale-state propagation
instruments.verification_check: Move one project through Problem Statement / Formulation Instrument; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.
